model_narrative: We are choosing which items to pack into a single knapsack. For each item we decide whether or not to include it. Every item carries a known profit if we take it and a known weight that it adds to the load. We also track the total profit collected from the items we choose. The objective is to maximize the total profit of the selected items.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the set of candidate items available to place into the knapsack, indexed by label

params.name: p
params.doc: the profit gained from including each item in the selection, in value units per item

params.name: w
params.doc: the weight each item contributes if it is included, in weight units per item

params.name: c
params.doc: the total weight capacity of the knapsack, in weight units; a single scalar

vars.name: x
vars.doc: selection indicator for each item; equals 1 if the item is placed in the knapsack and 0 otherwise

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total profit accumulated from the selected items, in value units; a single scalar

description: The items packed into the knapsack cannot weigh more than it can carry. Adding up the weight contributed by every item that is selected, the total must not exceed the knapsack's weight capacity.

model_narrative: We are choosing which items to pack into a single knapsack. For each item we decide whether or not to include it. The objective is to maximize the total profit of the selected items.

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total profit accumulated from the selected items, in value units; a single scalar

description: The total profit being tracked has to reflect exactly the items that were chosen. Summing the profit of every selected item, set the tracked total profit equal to that sum.

model_narrative: We are choosing which items to pack into a single knapsack. For each item we decide whether or not to include it. Every item carries a known profit if we take it and a known weight that it adds to the load. We also track the total profit collected from the items we choose. The objective is to maximize the total profit of the selected items.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the set of candidate items available to place into the knapsack, indexed by label

params.name: p
params.doc: the profit gained from including each item in the selection, in value units per item

params.name: w
params.doc: the weight each item contributes if it is included, in weight units per item

params.name: c
params.doc: the total weight capacity of the knapsack, in weight units; a single scalar

vars.name: x
vars.doc: selection indicator for each item; equals 1 if the item is placed in the knapsack and 0 otherwise

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total profit accumulated from the selected items, in value units; a single scalar

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, the combined weight of all the items chosen for the knapsack stays within its weight capacity. Finally, the tracked total profit equals the profit summed over every item that is selected.
